name: the primitive type called if

phase_0.boundary_condition: The holographic principle constraint where boundary information density limits bulk volume complexity.

VINIT.justification: The raw potential for dimensionality before any geometric projection or encoding occurs.

TANCH.justification: The terminal boundary that contains and defines the limits of the bulk information.

AFWD.justification: The forward mapping of higher-dimensional bulk data onto the lower-dimensional boundary manifold.

AREV.justification: The inverse mapping that recovers the internal bulk state from boundary data.

CLINK.justification: The sequential application of projection operators across nested dimensional layers.

IMSCRIB.justification: The identity state where the boundary correctly recognizes itself as the encoding of its own bulk.

FSPLIT.justification: The separation of a unified information state into a bulk volume and a boundary surface.

FFUSE.justification: The reunification of bulk and boundary into a single consistent information manifold.

EVALT.justification: The state where the boundary projection preserves all bulk information degrees of freedom.

EVALF.justification: The state where the boundary fails to capture the full complexity of the bulk volume.

ENGAGR.justification: The simultaneous existence of bulk and boundary as a single inseparable entity without resolution into one or the other.

IFIX.justification: The permanent fixation of the holographic mapping into a non-volatile memory state.

phase_3.void_description: A state of zero dimensionality where no information is localized or projected.
phase_3.true_description: A valid holographic mapping where the boundary successfully encodes the bulk.
phase_3.false_description: A mapping failure where the boundary-bulk correspondence is broken or lossy.
phase_3.both_description: A state of maximum criticality where bulk and boundary are indistinguishable.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the raw information potential before dimensional assignment.
Establish the identity of the information as a coherent manifold.
Bifurcate the manifold into a bulk volume and a boundary surface.
Affirm the lossless nature of the dimensional split.
Project the bulk degrees of freedom onto the boundary manifold.
Compose the projection with the boundary constraints.
Record the initial holographic encoding state to the topological store.
Attempt to reconstruct the bulk volume from the boundary data.
Identify any discrepancies or information loss during reconstruction.
Reintegrate the bulk and boundary into the original unified manifold.
Verify the self-consistency of the reconstructed manifold.
Anchor the system within the holographic screen boundary.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The holographic projection operator that maps bulk tensors to boundary states.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Non-local correlations between the boundary manifold and the bulk volume.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The boundary surface acting as a storage medium for bulk information.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The causal structure of the bulk determining the order of boundary updates.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Core.lean primitive definition for Dimensionality.if'.

phase_6.cycle_cost: One unit of area on the holographic screen per bit of bulk information.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: A unified information manifold with undefined dimensionality.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: A dimensionally split manifold with exact boundary-bulk correspondence.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The holographic mapping is a unitary transformation that preserves total information content.
